zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Farrar於1614年1月29日出生在英國藍卡爾波利時，他的父親Thomas Farrar37歲，母親Athellred Agnes Flournoy35歲。他於大約1642年與Elizabeth Mary Brocksip在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1694年2月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可琳享年80歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Farrar于1614年1月29日出生在英国藍卡爾波利时，他的父亲Thomas Farrar37岁，母亲Athellred Agnes Flournoy35岁。他于大约1642年与Elizabeth Mary Brocksip在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1694年2月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可琳享年80岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加。

nameMeanings.name: Farrar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): occupational name for a blacksmith or worker in iron. From Middle English fer(r)o(u)r, fer(r)er, fa(r)ro(u)r ‘ironworker, blacksmith’ (Old French ferreor, from medieval Latin ferrator, an agent derivative of ferrare ‘to shoe horses’, from ferrum ‘iron’, in medieval Latin ‘horseshoe’). Compare Ferrier and Farrow .

variants.name: Ferrer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Juan, Jorge, Carlos, Angel, Pedro, Roberto, Manuel, Ramon.
Catalan and southern French: from Latin ferrarius ‘ironworker, blacksmith’, either an occupational name or from the same word as an old personal name. This is the commonest Catalan surname.
English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Farrer
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
German: variant of   Forer   or   Fahrer  .

variants.name: Ferrar
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Ferro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Angelo, Mario, Pasquale, Alberto, Antonino, Bernardo, Guido, Carmine, Concetta, Dino, Ottavio, Pietro, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Carlos, Ramon, Eduardo, Fermin, Sixto, Angel.

Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, and Maltese: from ferro ‘iron’ (from Latin ferrum), used as a nickname denoting someone who was strong or as a metonymic occupational name for someone who produced or worked iron.
Italian: from a medieval personal name, preserved in the modern pet form Ferruccio.

variants.name: Fara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Eduardo, Carlos, Mario.
Italian (Sardinia): occupational name from Sardinian fara ‘messenger, courier, collector’.
Czech and Slovak (also Fára): topographic name from fara ‘priest's house’.

variants.name: Farrow
meanings: English (northern): hypercorrected form of Farrar , an occupational name for a smith or worker in iron. The original -ar or -er ending of this name came to be regarded as an error, and was changed to -ow.

variants.name: Farrah
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
Americanized form of Arabic   Farah  .

variants.name: Ferrier
meanings: Scottish and English: occupational name for a smith, one who shoed horses, from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, ironworker’ (medieval Latin ferrarius, from ferrus ‘horseshoe’, from Latin ferrum ‘iron’). Compare Farrier , Farrar .

Scottish and English: occupational name for a ferryman, from Middle English ferier(e) ‘ferryman’. Black reports that lands called Ferrylands in Dumbarton, by a ferry across the Clyde, belonged to Robert Ferrier in 1512.
French: occupational name from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, farrier’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Farra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ammar, Bassel, Fadi, Fadia, Farid, Hazem, Nabil, Nadim, Salem, Samir, Waseem, Yasser.  French Henri, Marcel.
English: variant of   Farrar  .
Arabic (the Levant): possibly a variant of   Farah  . Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians.

variants.name: Furrow
meanings: Probably an altered form of English Farrow (see Farrar ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: Salem Witch Trials
historicEvents.description: In 1692, the Salem Witch Trials began when Betty Parris fell sick with an unknown illness. Her cousin Abigail William claimed that she was being made sick because of witchcraft. As other strange events began to happen more of the young ladies in the town started accussing more people. These people mainly consisted of people the young ladies did not like. By the time the hysteria ended over 200 people were accussed of being witches. Nineteen men and women were found guilty and hung.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Biography- Remembering the Victims of the Salem Witch Executions

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Farrar was born on 29 January 1614, in Burnley, Lancashire, England, his father, Thomas Farrar, was 37 and his mother, Athellred Agnes Flournoy, was 35. He married Elizabeth Mary Brocksip about 1642, in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 23 February 1694, in Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 80, and was buried in British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Farrar est né le 29 janvier 1614, à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Farrar, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Athellred Agnes Flournoy, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Mary Brocksip environ 1642, à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 23 février 1694, à Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Farrar am 29. Januar 1614 in Burnley, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Farrar 37 und seine Mutter Athellred Agnes Flournoy 35. Er hat um 1642 in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Elizabeth Mary Brocksip geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Februar 1694 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in British Colonial America begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Farrarが1614年1月29日にイギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Farrarは37歳，母Athellred Agnes Flournoyは35歳であった。約1642年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーでElizabeth Mary Brocksipと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1694年2月23日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスリンで80歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Farrar(이)가 1614년 1월 29일 영국 랭카실르 번리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Farrar(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Athellred Agnes Flournoy(은)는 35세였다. 그는 대략 1642년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에서 Elizabeth Mary Brocksip(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1694년 2월 23일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 린에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Farrar родился 29 января 1614 г. в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Farrar было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Athellred Agnes Flournoy, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mary Brocksip около 1642 г. в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 23 февраля 1694 г. в местности линн, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Farrar nació el 29 de enero de 1614, en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Farrar, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Athellred Agnes Flournoy, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Mary Brocksip aproximadamente 1642, en Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 23 de febrero de 1694, en Lynn, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en British Colonial America.
